title: Anthropic $200B Google TPU Commitment Flows to Broadcom
summary: Anthropic's $200B/5GW Google Cloud commitment plus Broadcom's locked-in TPU supply role through 2031 makes AVGO the direct silicon beneficiary of the largest single AI compute contract ever disclosed.
catalyst: On May 5, 2026, The Information disclosed that Anthropic committed to spending roughly $200 billion over five years for 5 GW of Google Cloud and TPU capacity. This sits on top of Broadcom's April 6, 2026 8-K announcing a Long-Term Agreement to develop and supply Google's custom TPUs through 2031, plus an expanded Anthropic-Google-Broadcom collaboration giving Anthropic access to ~3.5 GW of next-generation TPU compute beginning 2027.
thesis: Google's TPUs are physically designed by Broadcom, which holds ~70% of the custom AI accelerator market and reports that TPU-related shipments are ~78% of its ASIC revenue. The Anthropic commitment forces Google to ramp TPU procurement at a pace only its co-design partner can fulfill, and Mizuho already models $21B of Anthropic-driven Broadcom revenue in 2026 and $42B in 2027 — sized against a $73B AI backlog and CEO Hock Tan's stated 'line of sight' to >$100B of AI silicon revenue in 2027.
body_md:
## Setup

Custom AI ASICs — chips co-designed for a single hyperscaler's workload rather than sold off the shelf — are the part of the AI buildout that is structurally pulling away from general-purpose GPUs. TrendForce projects ASIC AI server shipments will grow **44.6% in 2026 versus 16.1% for merchant GPUs**, lifting ASIC share of AI server shipments to a record 27.8% [1]. The driver is an inference-led regime where cost-per-token, power efficiency, and total cost of ownership matter more than peak throughput — exactly the axes purpose-built silicon dominates [1].

Two companies design essentially all of that silicon. Broadcom and Marvell together control roughly 95% of the ASIC co-design market, with Broadcom holding the leading ~70% share anchored by long-running programs with Google (TPU), Meta (MTIA), and now OpenAI [2][3]. HSBC analysis cited by industry coverage estimates that **TPU-related shipments account for approximately 78% of Broadcom's ASIC revenue**, making Google's compute roadmap the single most important variable in the AVGO P&L [4].

That roadmap just got a five-year, ten-figure floor.

## Why Now

Three disclosures in the past sixty days have rewritten the visibility on Broadcom's custom-silicon ramp.

First, on **April 6, 2026**, Broadcom filed an 8-K announcing a Long-Term Agreement under which Broadcom "will develop and supply custom Tensor Processing Units for Google's future generations of TPUs" along with a Supply Assurance Agreement for networking and rack components used in Google's next-generation AI systems — with terms extending **through 2031** [5]. The same filing disclosed an expanded collaboration with Google and Anthropic in which **Anthropic will access approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU-based compute through Broadcom beginning in 2027** [5][6]. The financial terms were withheld, but the structural commitment is now public and binding.

Second, on **May 5, 2026**, The Information reported — and multiple outlets confirmed — that Anthropic agreed to spend roughly **$200 billion over five years on Google Cloud and TPU compute, equating to ~5 GW of capacity** [7][8]. Alphabet simultaneously agreed to invest at least $10 billion in Anthropic, scalable to $40 billion on performance milestones [7]. Google's TPU is the silicon underneath that $200B; Broadcom is the silicon partner that builds it.

Third, **Broadcom's own results have caught up to the order book**. Q1 FY2026 (reported March 6, 2026) delivered $19.31B revenue (+29% YoY) and AI semiconductor revenue of **$8.4B, up 106% YoY** — driven by custom AI accelerators and AI networking [9]. Q2 FY2026 guidance is $22.0B in total revenue (+47% YoY, well above the $20.5B consensus) with AI semiconductor revenue guided to **$10.7B, +140% YoY** [9][10]. CEO Hock Tan stated on the Q1 call that the company has "line of sight" to more than **$100B in AI silicon revenue in 2027**, supported by ramps across Google, Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI [10].

The Anthropic commitment removes the largest swing variable from that 2027 number. Mizuho's published model already places **$21B of Broadcom revenue from Anthropic in 2026 and $42B in 2027** — which alone would deliver roughly half of the $100B 2027 AI target [11][12].

## Company-Specific Edge — Broadcom (AVGO)

Broadcom is not a generic chip vendor riding the AI tide; it is the contractually embedded silicon partner for two of the four entities at the center of frontier-model compute (Google and Meta, with Anthropic now layered on through Google, and OpenAI added via the October 2025 10 GW co-development deal) [3][13].

The co-design model is what makes the moat durable. Broadcom owns the physical implementation, advanced packaging (3.5D), high-speed SerDes, Tomahawk/Jericho networking silicon, and the systems-engineering work to integrate custom XPUs into hyperscaler rack architectures. A customer can announce it wants to "design its own chip," but it cannot in practice swap out the partner that has the 2nm and 3nm tape-outs, the HBM4 interface IP, and the packaging slots at TSMC. The Google 8-K — a multi-year supply assurance through 2031 — is the legal expression of that lock-in [5].

Financially, Broadcom screens as one of the highest-quality megacaps in the IOWN universe. Per the screener report (May 11, 2026), AVGO scores **80 (BUY)** with Excellence Infrastructure at 10/10, AI Resilience at 10/10 (IMMUNE classification — the company builds the silicon AI runs on), and an Infinite Game score of 6 (MIXED) [14]. Profit margin of 36.6%, ROE of 33.4%, and revenue growth of 29.5% YoY sit at the top end of the screened universe, and a forward PEG of 0.94 indicates the market is still underpricing the growth path relative to current earnings trajectory [14].

Adjacent: Marvell (MRVL, screener BUY, 86) is the secondary beneficiary as the #2 custom ASIC house, with Amazon Trainium and Microsoft engagements projected to reach $9-11B in AI ASIC revenue in 2026 [2][3]. MRVL is already the subject of the ai-optical-interconnects opportunity, so this thesis is kept ticker-focused on AVGO to avoid double-counting.

## Numbers That Matter

**Broadcom (AVGO) — May 29, 2026 close: $446.77** [15]

From the screener report (data screened 2026-05-11) [14]:
- Forward P/E: 23.7 | PEG: 0.94 | TTM P/E: 84.0
- Revenue growth YoY: 29.5% | 5-yr CAGR: ~18.4%
- EPS growth YoY: 31.6%
- Profit margin: 36.6% | ROE: 33.4% | D/E: 82.7%
- Dividend yield: 0.6% | 16 consecutive years paid, 14 years of growth
- IOWN Overall Score: 80 (BUY), AI Resilience: 10 (LOW RISK), Infrastructure: 10

AI franchise (from company-reported and analyst-modeled figures):
- Q1 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue: **$8.4B (+106% YoY)** [9]
- Q2 FY2026 AI guidance: **$10.7B (+140% YoY)** [10]
- AI-specific order backlog: **~$73B** [10][16]
- 2027 AI revenue target (management): **>$100B** [10]
- Mizuho modeled Anthropic contribution: **$21B (2026), $42B (2027)** [11]
- Google TPU share of AVGO ASIC revenue (HSBC estimate): **~78%** [4]
- Custom AI accelerator market share: **~70%** [2]

Market structure:
- ASIC AI server shipment growth 2026: **+44.6% YoY vs +16.1% for GPUs** [1]
- ASIC share of AI server shipments 2026: **27.8% (record since 2023)** [1]

## Bear Case

**Anthropic execution risk.** The 8-K explicitly states that Anthropic's consumption of the expanded compute is *contingent on its continued commercial success* [5]. Anthropic's revenue run rate has crossed $30B [6], but the $200B commitment is an order of magnitude larger than current revenue — sustaining that ramp requires Claude and Anthropic API monetization to continue compounding at 2025-2026 rates. If frontier-model demand plateaus or unit economics deteriorate, Anthropic could renegotiate or stretch the timeline, and Broadcom's modeled Anthropic revenue would compress.

**Customer concentration.** ~70% of Broadcom's 2026 AI revenue and the 2027 pipeline come from three customers — Google, Anthropic, and Meta [4]. Any single relationship souring (e.g., Google deciding to bring more TPU implementation in-house at a future node) materially impairs the thesis. Mitigating: the multi-year supply assurance through 2031, the multi-generational nature of TPU co-design, and the fact that Google has not in-housed TPU physical design for over a decade despite owning Alphabet.

**Margin pressure from scale.** Several of these customers are sophisticated buyers who will press for unit-economic concessions as volumes scale. Broadcom's 68% adjusted EBITDA margin in Q1 FY2026 [9] could compress if AI custom-silicon mix shifts toward lower-margin foundry pass-through.

**Power and packaging bottlenecks.** Broadcom is itself constrained by TSMC's 3nm/2nm packaging capacity and by hyperscalers' ability to physically build the gigawatts of data-center capacity these chips require. The bottleneck thesis already covered in *ai-power-bottleneck* and *data-center-grid-buildout* is the real-economy gate on whether 5 GW of Anthropic compute can actually be deployed on the contract's stated timeline.

**Valuation.** Trailing P/E of 84 embeds significant growth expectations. The forward P/E of 23.7 and PEG of 0.94 indicate the multiple is supported on forward earnings, but a meaningful deceleration in AI revenue from the +106%/+140% YoY pace would trigger multiple compression even if absolute earnings continued to grow.

## Conclusion

The AI hyperscaler buildout has reached a stage where the question is no longer 'is the spending real?' but 'who is contractually positioned to capture it?' For custom AI silicon — the fastest-growing slice of the buildout, where ASIC shipment growth is running ~3x merchant GPU growth — the answer has been narrowing for two years, and the Anthropic-Google-Broadcom announcements in April and May 2026 essentially closed the question. Broadcom is the silicon partner of record for Google's TPU roadmap through 2031, the silicon partner for the 3.5 GW of next-gen TPU compute Anthropic will draw down from 2027, and one of two co-development partners on OpenAI's 10 GW custom-chip program [5][13]. The financial expression is a 47% revenue growth guide for Q2 FY2026, a $73B AI backlog, and a credible $100B 2027 AI revenue target with Mizuho already modeling Anthropic alone at $42B of that figure [10][11]. At a forward P/E of 23.7 and PEG of 0.94 [14], the market is pricing Broadcom as a high-quality cyclical, not as the contractually locked-in beneficiary of the largest concentrated AI infrastructure cycle in the industry's history. That asymmetry is the trade.

ticker_rationale.AVGO: Broadcom designs Google's TPUs (HSBC: ~78% of AVGO ASIC revenue), holds ~70% custom AI accelerator market share, and just locked in a supply agreement through 2031 with Google and a 3.5 GW Anthropic compute commitment from 2027. Q2 FY2026 AI revenue guided to $10.7B (+140% YoY), $73B AI backlog, screener BUY at score 80 with forward PEG of 0.94 and 36.6% profit margin.

risks: Anthropic's $200B/5GW commitment is explicitly contingent in the 8-K on 'continued commercial success' — if Claude monetization or enterprise API ramp slows materially, Anthropic could renegotiate or stretch the take-or-pay schedule and compress Mizuho's modeled $42B 2027 Broadcom contribution

Customer concentration risk: Google + Anthropic + Meta together represent ~70% of Broadcom's 2026 AI revenue and 2027 pipeline; loss or in-housing of TPU physical-design work at Google would impair the core thesis

Packaging and power constraints (TSMC 3nm/2nm advanced packaging slots, data-center power availability) could cap how much of the 5 GW Anthropic commitment can actually be delivered on the contracted timeline, pushing revenue out beyond 2027-2028
Trailing P/E of 84 embeds significant growth expectations; AI revenue growth slowing from +106%/+140% YoY pace to even +50% would trigger meaningful multiple compression
invalidation: Broadcom AI semiconductor revenue growth falls below 40% YoY for two consecutive quarters (vs. current +106%/+140% trajectory), indicating loss of contracted ramp or share to in-house customer designs
Google publicly announces in-housing of TPU physical design or moves a future TPU generation (TPU v8 or beyond) away from Broadcom co-design, contradicting the 2031 Supply Assurance Agreement

Anthropic announces a material restructuring or reduction of its Google/Broadcom compute commitment, or shifts >25% of its committed capacity to a non-Broadcom supplier (AWS Trainium via Marvell, Microsoft Maia, in-house, etc.)
Broadcom AI semiconductor backlog ($73B disclosed) declines for two consecutive quarters, signaling slowing forward demand visibility
Forward P/E expands above 35x with no corresponding upward revision to FY2027 AI revenue guidance, indicating speculative re-rating disconnected from contracted earnings power
